DRAFT FOR LEGAL REVIEW · not yet in force

Last revised 13 June 2026 · draft

Cold Outreach Privacy Notice

This notice is the draft statutory disclosure under UK GDPR Articles 13 and 14, read with the Data Protection Act 2018, for individuals contacted by Tamazia through research-led outbound communication. Because the personal data used for outreach is, in most cases, obtained from a source other than the individual, the Article 14 disclosure points apply alongside the Article 13 points, and both are mapped below. This document is a draft pending legal review. Until it is reviewed, the live disclosure is the data protection notice together with the short notice carried in the first email Tamazia sends.

1. Identity and contact details of the controller (Articles 13(1)(a) and 14(1)(a))

The controller of the personal data described in this notice is [TODO: founder: registered company name], a company registered in England and Wales under company number [TODO: founder: company registration number], whose registered office is at [TODO: founder: registered office address], trading as Tamazia.

For all data protection matters, including any of the requests described in section 8, the contact point is dpo@tamazia.co.uk. To object to outreach specifically, the contact point is founder@tamazia.co.uk, or a reply of STOP to any message.

2. ICO registration

Tamazia is, or is in the course of becoming, registered with the UK Information Commissioner's Office as a data controller. Registration reference: [TODO: founder: ICO registration number]. This reference is published here once the registration is confirmed.

3. Categories of personal data (Article 14(1)(d))

Tamazia processes business contact data only. The categories are: full name, business email address, job or role title, and the name of the current employer. Where a business telephone number or a public professional profile URL is held, it falls within the same category. Tamazia does not seek or process special category data through outreach.

4. Source of the personal data (Article 14(2)(f))

Because the data is, in most cases, not obtained directly from the individual, Article 14 requires Tamazia to state its source. The personal data is obtained from publicly accessible sources and from verified business data providers. Public sources include company websites, public professional profiles, and public registers. Data providers include business enrichment platforms. The specific source is recorded against each individual record. Tamazia does not obtain data from a source it knows to be unlawful.

5. Purposes and lawful basis (Articles 13(1)(c) and 14(1)(c))

The purpose of the processing is to assess whether Tamazia's compliance-led SEO, generative engine optimisation, and content services are relevant to a senior commercial decision-maker within a stated target sector, and, where relevant, to make a single piece of research-led contact on that basis.

The lawful basis is legitimate interests under Article 6(1)(f) UK GDPR. The legitimate interest is the promotion of business-to-business professional services to the individuals most likely to be responsible for the decision. A legitimate interests assessment, balancing that interest against the rights and interests of the individual, is held on file and is available to the individual on request. Where outreach is conducted by electronic mail, Tamazia also complies with the Privacy and Electronic Communications Regulations 2003.

6. Recipients of the personal data (Articles 13(1)(e) and 14(1)(e))

The personal data is handled by Tamazia and by processors acting on Tamazia's documented instructions. These include email delivery and routing providers, the scheduling provider Cal.com, Inc. (United States, certified under the Data Privacy Framework including its UK extension) for strategy-call bookings, email verification providers used before a message is sent, and infrastructure and hosting providers. Each processor is bound by a data processing agreement and, where personal data is transferred outside the United Kingdom, by the UK International Data Transfer Addendum to the European Commission Standard Contractual Clauses or another lawful transfer mechanism. The current list is published at the sub-processors page.

7. Retention period (Articles 13(2)(a) and 14(2)(a))

The personal data is held until the individual asks for it to be erased, or until twenty-four months after the last meaningful interaction, whichever is earlier. Where an individual objects to outreach or asks to be suppressed, a minimal record (such as the email address on a suppression list) is retained for as long as necessary to honour that objection, and for no other purpose.

8. Rights of the individual (Articles 13(2)(b) and 14(2)(c))

The individual has the right to be informed, the right of access to their personal data, the right to rectification of inaccurate data, the right to erasure, the right to restriction of processing, the right to data portability where it applies, and the right to object.

Because the lawful basis is legitimate interests, the right to object under Article 21 UK GDPR applies. On objection to processing for the purpose described in this notice, Tamazia stops that processing. The individual may object, or ask for erasure, at any time and without giving a reason, by replying STOP to any message or by writing to founder@tamazia.co.uk or dpo@tamazia.co.uk. There is no charge for exercising these rights.

9. Right to complain to the supervisory authority (Articles 13(2)(d) and 14(2)(e))

The individual has the right to lodge a complaint with the UK Information Commissioner's Office at any time. The Information Commissioner's Office can be reached at Wycliffe House, Water Lane, Wilmslow, Cheshire, SK9 5AF, United Kingdom, by telephone on +44 303 123 1113, and online at ico.org.uk/make-a-complaint. Tamazia would welcome the chance to address a concern directly before a complaint is made, but the right to complain to the Information Commissioner's Office is not conditional on doing so.

10. When and how this notice is provided (Article 14(3))

Where personal data is obtained from a source other than the individual, this notice, in summary form, is provided at the latest at the time of the first communication, and in any event within one month of obtaining the data. The summary notice is carried in the first outbound email, with a link to this page for the full disclosure. No automated decision-making producing legal or similarly significant effects within the meaning of Article 22 UK GDPR is carried out as part of outreach.

Cookies, with your consent.

Strictly necessary cookies run the site; optional analytics only with your consent. Cookie policy · Global Privacy Control detected and honoured.
